stories.title: The Pirate's Last Voyage
stories.description: Set sail on stormy seas in search of cursed treasure.
stories.text:
The wind filled the sails as the ship cut through the dark water. Waves crashed against the hull. The captain stood at the wheel, his coat whipping in the storm. Thunder rumbled across the open sea.

"All hands!" he shouted above the roar of the wind. The crew scrambled across the deck. Their footsteps hammered on the wet planks. Rain lashed down from black clouds. Lightning split the sky and struck the water with a crack.

The helmsman gripped the wheel. Water crashed over the bow and dripped from the rigging. An arrow whistled past from the pursuing ship behind them. Then another. Arrows flew through the wind and rain.

The captain drew his sword. "Ready the cannons!" he roared. Fire crackled along the fuse lines. The cannons exploded with a thundering blast that shook the deck. Smoke and flames erupted across the enemy vessel.

The ships drew alongside. Swords clashed as pirates leapt across the gap. The strike of blade against blade rang through the storm. A shield blocked a heavy blow. The fighter shouted a battle cry and pushed forward.

Glass shattered as a lantern fell from the mast. Fire spread across the enemy deck. Flames climbed the rigging. The beast of war raged between the two ships. A dragon figurehead cracked and trembled as the bow splintered.

The captain crept below deck on the enemy ship. His footsteps echoed in the dark hold. Water dripped through the planks above. He found it: a chest of gold, cursed by an ancient witch. Magic seemed to hum from within. A crystal skull sat atop the pile, its eyes flickering with an arcane glow.

He grabbed the chest and climbed. On deck, the storm began to pass. Thunder faded into the distance. The wind settled. The fire dimmed to embers on the broken ship.

As they sailed into calmer water, silence fell over the crew. The rain stopped. His heartbeat slowed. Then a cheer rose from the deck. The crowd celebrated with shouts and song. Music drifted across the waves. Bells rang from the lookout post. The captain raised his sword to the sky and the crew shouted their triumph into the night.
